cleanTransientStorage().details: This function removes the transient allowances, which could be useful for         integration with Account Abstraction when bundling several UserOps calling InputVerifier.

defineNewContext(address[],uint256).details: Only the owner can set a new context.

params.newSignersSet: The new set of signers to be set. This array should not be empty and without duplicates nor null values.
params.newThreshold: The threshold to be set. Threshold should be non-null and less than the number of signers.

getCoprocessorSigners().details: If there are too many signers, it could be out-of-gas.

returns._0: uint8 The current version for new handles.
string Name and the version of the contract.
isSigner     Whether the account is a valid signer.

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC-1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

setThreshold(uint256).details: Only the owner can set a threshold.

params.threshold: The threshold to be set. Threshold should be non-null and less than the number of signers.

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

EIP712_INPUT_VERIFICATION_TYPE().notice: The definition of the CiphertextVerification structure typed data.

EIP712_INPUT_VERIFICATION_TYPEHASH().notice: The hash of the CiphertextVerification structure typed data definition used for signature validation.

defineNewContext(address[],uint256).notice: Sets a new context (i.e. new set of unique signers and new threshold).

getVersion().notice: Getter for the name and version of the contract.

isSigner(address).notice: Returns whether the account address is a valid signer.

setThreshold(uint256).notice: Sets a threshold (i.e. the minimum number of valid signatures required to accept a transaction).
